UG Programme in
Technology and Business Management

A 3+ 1 Dual Degree Programme with Illinois Tech where you learn business by doing business.
See how we take business education to a global scale.

Apply Now

Undergraduate Programme in
Technology & Business Management

Format

Full Time

On campus in
Gurugram & Chicago

Commencement Date

August ‘26

Round 3 Deadline: 13th May '26

Eligibility

Class XIIth

Current Students &
Pass-outs (2024 onwards)

Duration

4 Years

3 Years at Masters' Union & 1 Year at Illinois Tech

Round 3 Starting Soon Apply Now
  • Highlights
  • Curriculum & Faculty
  • Admissions & Fees
  • Career Prospects
  • Class Profile

3+1 Dual Degree
Study Abroad Programme

Discover how 3+1 Dual Degree Programme with Illinois Tech enables
our students to study in Chicago & earn a globally recognised
Bachelor’s of Science in Business Administration.

World-Class
Business Education

Our course is led by industry titans from renowned companies like Google,
Microsoft, Amazon, and more, giving you the insights and skills necessary to
succeed on an international scale.

Learn from Top CXOs & MDs

Engage with over 200 senior executives from leading global
companies like Google, Microsoft, Meta, Kia, Epigamia, and
more.

1

Real-time case studies from industry leaders

2

Direct mentorship from executives at top MNCs

3

Industry-led workshops & projects with international focus

Build and Scale Your
International Venture

Launch your e-commerce business and target ₹1 Crore+ in
revenue, reaching customers worldwide.

1

₹3 Cr+ revenue generated by UG Ventures

2

60+ startups launched

3

10k+ customers across countries

Download Dropshipping Report

Work with Leading
International Brands

Gain real-world experience from day one with top international companies.

1

Intern with firms like Nestle, KPMG, Snapchat

2

75% students intern within 1st year of college

3

Work across India, UAE, US, Singapore

Download Internship Report

Gain Global Exposure with
Dual Degree from Illinois Tech

Complete your B.S. in Business Administration from Illinois
Institute of Technology (USA), and expand your career network
worldwide.

1

Study abroad in Chicago’s influential business hub

2

Leverage Illinois Tech’s worldwide internship network

3

Connect with alumni network across 3 continents

Download GIP Report

from Global Giants

Real Insights from
Our Global Faculty

Gain valuable insights into how businesses are built across different industries, with
mentorship from leaders who’ve shaped global markets.

Richard Pattle, Co-founder & CEO, True Beacon

Spot high-growth markets, build cross-border ventures, &
leverage opportunities shaped by reverse brain drain in India.

Dr. Edward Rogers, NASA Veteran

Learn how a NASA Veteran, brings his simulation-driven
curriculum to teach the art of managing complexities

Kunal Bahl, Former CEO, Snapdeal

Survive early-stage chaos, time your launch right & build a ₹100 crore business without chasing funding

Karan Johar, Filmmaker & Entrepreneur

Learn how India’s top filmmaker built Dharma navigating budgets, box office risks, & the business behind blockbusters

Varun Mayya, Entrepreneur & Content Creator

Discover the impact of Gen-AI on content creation & how you
can leverage AI to transform your career with Varun Mayya

Naveen Munjal, MD, Hero Electric

Navigate the high-voltage opportunities in the electric vehicle
industry with Mr. Naveen Munjal, MD of Hero Electric

Graduate with a Prestigious
Degree from US

Graduate with a Prestigious
Degree from US

Study Abroad at
Illinois Tech, Chicago

  • 1

    Spend 3 years in India followed by 4th year at  Illinois Tech, Chicago

  • 2

    Graduate from Illinois Tech, #1 in Illinois and #20 in the US by New York Times*

  • 3

    Unlock stellar placement opportunities and higher studies options in the US.

  • 4

    Earn Certification in Tech & Business Management from Masters’ Union.

    • *Based on the New York Times college ranking tool, 2023, for high earning and economic mobility.

Real World
Experience from Day One

Over 80 students have interned at top global brands like
Nestle, EY, Indigo, Marks & Spencer and more.

Watch Video

Download Internship Report

Student Internship Experience

Download Internship Report

Jeet Shah

Brand Marketing Intern, IndiGo

Class of 2027

“I landed my dream internship at IndiGo in my first year, where I contributed to launching their business class and loyalty program. This experience provided me with valuable insights into airline marketing and customer engagement. I have been passionate about aviation since childhood and my passion further deepened after meeting Dr. Anand Srinivasan, Co-founder of Akasa Air, during his tour at Masters’ Union, gaining firsthand industry insights”

1/4

Sabarna Jana

Marketing Intern, Epigamia

Class of 2027

“During my internship at Epigamia, I was praised for my market research and identifying the right product-market fit – skills I honed in the marketing class taught by Mr. Bhupesh Manohar. I also analyzed raw data with precision with advanced excel, making the experience both practical and enriching.”

2/4

Gyan Banjan

Business Development Intern, Zerope

Class of 2027

“My time at ZeroPe offered a front-row view of India's fintech transformation. I explored consumer behavior in healthcare spending and successfully converted a previously unreachable client. Tasked by Ashneer Grover to onboard 20 healthcare partners in two months, I applied the Need Gap strategy from one of our classes, which helped me close 44 leads in six weeks, earning the position of top performer and an extended offer to join the team full-time.”

3/4

Dhruv Lakra

Product Intern, BluSmart

Class of 2027

“During my internship at BluSmart, I developed expertise in B2B sales, marketing, and social media. My experience in Dropshipping Mela helped me understand the business requirements quickly and I also honed skills in collaboration, analytics, and business building”

4/4

Roghan

Founder’s Office Intern, Basil

Class of 2027

“I Interned at Basil’s Founder’s Office, where I crafted marketing strategies, closed deals with HP and Dyninno, and developed Basil Wallet as a product for these companies. With GTM strategies taught to us, I drove impactful marketing. The communication training from Harvard Professor Mihir Mankad equipped me to lead my team for two months”

4/4
Download Internship Report

Learn by Doing
Not Just by Sitting in Class

Students dive into real-world challenges that span multiple industries across borders through challenges like Dropshipping, Industry Treks, and Content Creation.

Study Abroad Programme

Study Abroad to-
Gain Global Exposure

Study abroad for summer school or short duration immersion at
prestigious global B-Schools like Babson College, IESEG France,
SDA Bocconi, Harvard etc and gain unmatched insights, while
experiencing life in a new country.

Dropshipping Challenge

Scale Your E-commerce Venture
to ₹1Cr+ Globally

Build and scale your e-commerce business with an
international customer base, aiming for ₹1 Crore+ in
revenue.

Download Dropshipping Report

Content Creator Challenge

Become an Influencer with
Worldwide Reach

Develop your personal brand and grow a global following,
creating channels with thousands, even millions, of
followers.

MEET OUR CREATOR ALUMS

Cospire

@cospireEdu

pranjalsagrawal

@pranjalsagrawal

Rajas Gupta

@rajasguptayt

shutupshenoy

@shutupshenoy
Download Content Creator Challenge Report

Startup Weekend

Innovate and Prototype
in Just 48 Hours

Work with peers to create global-scale prototypes and
solutions, exchanging ideas, and getting mentored.

  • AI/ML Hackathon
  • Microsoft One Day Challenge
  • Product Management Hackathon
  • boAt Marketing Challenge

Student Investment Fund

Run Your Own
5Cr Investment Fund

Get the chance to oversee investments across diverse
asset classes, including Crypto and Blockchain, Public and
Private Equities, Fund of Funds, REITs, InvITs, and exciting
startups.

Investments in Public Equities

Poonawalla Group, ITC, CDSL

AlphaMint Labs, BluSmart

Glimpse of Student Life

Engage With a
Future-Ready Curriculum

A powerful blend of Masters’ Union’s experiential learning and Illinois Tech’s
academic rigor, designed to equip you with the skills, mindset, and global
outlook to lead in tomorrow’s business world.

Enabling You to Excel
Inside and Outside the Classroom

  • CA PATHWAY
  • CFA PATHWAY

Learn the Foundations of Technology & Business

Master the building blocks of business and tech: accounting, economics, problem solving and core data visualisation skills.

  • In Class : Core Courses
  • Out Class
  • Art of Communication: How to give an inspiring speech?
  • Intro to PowerPoint & Excel: How to get comfortable with Excel and Slides?
  • Legalities in Business: How does law impact business decisions?
  • Fundamentals of Accounting: How to read and analyse financial statements?
  • Macroeconomics: How does a country's economy work?
  • Microeconomics: How does the Micro Economy work?
  • Problem Solving and Structuring: How to stop guessing and start solving?
  • Managerial Accounting: How to tell if your unit economics are lying to you?
  • Introduction to Product Management: How to develop a product mindset?
  • Building Marketing Strategies: How to develop a GTM strategy using funnels?
  • Fundamentals of Statistics: How to use stats to build a better business?
  • Mastering Data Visualisation: How to use Tableau and SQL to visualise data?
  • Dropshipping Challenge: Build your own D2C brand from scratch, selling across online and offline locations, resulting in revenue of INR 10L+.
  • Global Immersion Programme: Travel to a country you’ve never been to before and immerse in its tech, business, innovation, and automotive industries. Destinations include Japan, Singapore, Germany, and Silicon Valley.
  • Shopfloor Internship: Gain hands-on experience by working directly on the ground in manufacturing or operational settings, learning the intricacies of business processes, problem-solving, and real-world applications.

Develop New-Age Skills that Power Modern Business

Advance into next-generation capabilities: product analytics, brand & digital marketing, venture capital and corporate finance.

  • In Class : Core Courses
  • Out Class
  • Managerial Behaviour: How to become the leader everyone aspires to be?
  • Supply Chain & Logistics: How to procure & ship globally?
  • Introduction to HRM: How to work effectively in teams?
  • Corporate Strategies: How to think strategically about your business?
  • Corporate Governance: How can governance shape strategic choices?
  • Corporate Finance: How to allocate budgets and control costs?
  • Venture Capital and Fintech: How do VC and fintech firms work?
  • Design Thinking & Prototyping: How to use design thinking to build effective products?
  • Product Analytics: How to use product analytics for deeper insights?
  • Digital Marketing: How to run simple ads on Tiktok, Meta, & Google?
  • Consumer Behaviour: How to position a brand using consumer psychology & behaviour?
  • Intro to Machine Learning-I: How to learn the math & logic that powers every AI tool?
  • Content Creation Challenge: Build and grow a digital presence on platforms like YouTube or Instagram, aiming to cross 5,000 followers while learning to strategise, produce & optimise content like a Creator-preneur
  • Corporate Internship: Intern with leading companies to gain first hand exposure to industry practices & build leadership, communication & stakeholder management skills
  • Global Immersion Programme: Visit and study in a new country, understanding its businesses, culture, and social systems.

Transform Into a Business Leader Through Real-World Immersion

Dive into capstone projects, complex case simulations, and interdisciplinary labs that mirror real business challenges.

  • In Class : Core Courses
  • Out Class
  • Intro to New Age Technologies: How to master prompt engineering to leverage Gen AI
  • Public Policy and Advocacy: How does public policy influence business decisions?
  • The Founder’s Playbook: How to craft strategy like a founder?
  • Negotiation : How to create a win-win situation?
  • Valuations & Financial Modelling: How to value business?
  • Markets, Portfolios & Prudence: How to invest in capital markets & build a portfolio?
  • Markets, Portfolios & Prudence: How to invest in capital markets & build a portfolio?
  • Product Led Growth Strategies: How to drive product led growth?
  • Product Strategy and Innovation: How to build habit-forming products?
  • Strategic Brand Management: How to craft a brand like Nike using voice, tone, identity?
  • Content Generation & Advertising: How to create content for YouTube & Instagram?
  • Big Data Analytics: How to use big data to drive decision-making?
  • Intro to Machine Learning Part 2: How to leverage machine learning to build solutions?
  • Industry Treks: Visit startup VC firms and large-scale companies to interact with founders, operators, and investors, gaining first hand insight into industry dynamics.
  • Leadership retreats: Learn the art of leadership through a 7-10 day offsite retreat with uncertain and ever changing environments, and get trained in complex decision making.
  • Venture Initiation Programme: Go through a complete startup journey - building businesses from scratch, generating revenue, and pitching to real investors

Launch Your Global Career from Chicago

Study in Chicago, work on real-world projects, and graduate with a U.S. business degree and a network that spans continents.

  • Study Abroad
  • Indirect Tax Laws: How to ensure efficiency in indirect taxation systems?
  • Integrated Business Solutions: How to tie strategy, finance, tax, and ethics together?
  • Direct Tax Laws & International Taxation: How to manage corporate & cross-border taxation?
  • Spend a Year at Illinois Tech: Study for your 4th year in Chicago and graduate with a Bachelor of Science in Business Administration from Illinois Tech, US.
    Check Detailed Curriculum here

Build the Foundations of Finance and Economics

Master the fundamentals of accounting, micro and macro economics, statistics, and ethics, laying the groundwork to understand how businesses, markets, and financial systems function.

  • In Class : Core Courses
  • Hands-on Projects
  • Microeconomics : How to predict demand and supply?
  • Fundamentals of Accounting : How to read financials like an investor?
  • Macroeconomics : How a country’s economy work?
  • Business Statistics : How to decode the signal from the noise?
  • Introduction to Stock Markets : How to identify the winning stocks?
  • Excel for Finance : How to structure financial data for modelling?
  • Fixed Income : How to price bonds and understand investor confidence?
  • Quantitative Methods I : How to turn financial data into profit-making insights?
  • Corporate Finance I : How to optimise capital allocation and cost of capital?
  • Art of Communication : How to pitch, persuade, & inspire like a leader?
  • AI Tools in Trading : How to leverage AI and boost productivity?
  • Design a Winning Stock-Portfolio Using Technical Analysis
  • Perform Forensic Analysis of a Company’s Financial Statements
  • Build a Predictive Inventory Model for a Client in Manufacturing Industry

Master the Core of Investment and Valuation

Deepen your knowledge with corporate finance, fixed income, equities, and derivatives. Learn to analyze markets, build financial models, and make data-driven investment decisions.

  • In Class : Core Courses
  • Hands-on Projects
  • Corporate Finance II : How to manage a company like a CFO?
  • Quantitative Methods II : How to use futures and options to hedge risk?
  • Alternative Investments I : How to leverage REITs and commodities for diversification?
  • Financial Reporting and Analysis I : How to analyse ratios and footnotes?
  • Financial Reporting and Analysis II : How to interpret assets, efficiency, and growth?
  • Ethics & Professional Standards : How to judge integrity and governance mechanisms?
  • Portfolio Management : How to turn uncertainty into opportunity?
  • Sustainable Finance & ESG Investing : How to price risk & opportunity sustainably?
  • Private Equity & Venture Capital : How to fuel innovation through private capital?
  • Investment Banking : How to identify opportunities and close deals?
  • Industry Primers : How to understand ways of working across industries?
  • Prepare and Analyse Full Financial Statements for a Public Company
  • Use Hypothesis Testing to Identify Alpha in Equity Returns
  • Perform Ratio and Valuation Analysis Using Adjusted Financial Statements
  • Construct and Optimise a Diversified Portfolio Using CAPM Principles

Advance into Risk, Strategy, and Asset Management

Explore advanced derivatives, alternative investments, and risk management while tackling CFA-aligned projects. Learn to manage portfolios and assess investment strategies.

  • In Class : Core Courses
  • Hands-on Projects
  • Fixed Income II : How to navigate interest rate risks?
  • Quantitative Methods III : How to quantify market signals and build winning strategies?
  • Economics for Investment Management : How to drive strategic investing & risk control?
  • Investment Exit Strategies : How to plan, structure & close investment exits?
  • Equity Valuation : How to assess a company's value and optimize investments?
  • Advanced Financial Reporting & Analysis : How to use reports to assess performance?
  • Corporate Issuers : How to optimize corporate debt and equity issuances?
  • Portfolio Management & Wealth Planning: How to evaluate, adjust, & optimise portfolios?
  • Derivatives II : How to hedge against uncertainty without losing upside?
  • Ethics & Professional Standards II : How to review existing professional standards?
  • Alternative Investments II : How to multiply returns and manage volatility?
  • Develop a Time-Series Model to Forecast Asset Returns
  • Value an Options Portfolio Using the Black–Scholes Model
  • Design and Backtest an Active Multi-Factor Equity Strategy
  • Recommend an Optimal Capital Structure and Dividend Policy for a Public Firm

Transition from Analyst to Investment Professional

Apply your learning through internships and capstones, mastering portfolio construction, ethics, and compliance, preparing to operate as a finance professional in global markets.

  • In Class : Core Courses
  • Hands-on Projects
  • Advanced Portfolio Management Strategies : How to optimize for alpha and beta?
  • Portfolio Construction : How to balance growth and stability in your portfolio?
  • Portfolio Performance & Attribution : How to use attribution for investment decisions?
  • Derivatives & Risk Management : How to implement risk management strategies?
  • Ethics & Professional Standards III : How to integrate ethics into investment strategies?
  • Asset Allocation : How to strategically allocate assets for long-term success?
  • Construct and Optimise an Institutional Portfolio Based on Risk–Return Objectives
  • Design and Backtest a Hedging Strategy Using Options and Futures
  • Build and Rebalance a Multi-Asset Portfolio Incorporating Alternative Investments
  • Develop a Strategic Asset Allocation Framework Under Different Market Scenarios

Curriculum with
Unique In Class and Out Class Combination

  • Year 1

    Learn Fundamentals of Tech and Business

  • Year 2

    Excel in New-Age Skills

  • Year 3

    Transform into a Business Leader

  • Year 4

    Get Ready for the Real World

In Class | Core Courses

Foundational Courses

  • Art of Communication - Public Speaking & Elocution

  • Introduction to Microsoft PowerPoint & Excel

  • Legalities in Business - From Compliances to Loopholes

  • Fundamentals of Accounting - Understanding Financial Statements


  • Macroeconomics - How Economy Affects Business

  • Microeconomics - Understanding the Dynamics of Demand and Supply

    • Strategic Management

  • Problem Solving and Structuring - How Leaders Make Decisions



    • Finance and Fintech

  • Managerial Accounting - Diagnosing Business Health

    • Product Management

  • Introduction to Product Management - How to Build an App




    • Digital Marketing and Brand Management

  • Building Marketing Strategies - Getting Customers to Buy the Product

    • Data Analytics

  • Principles of Statistics - Navigating Uncertainties in Business

  • Mastering Data Visualisation - Telling a Story with Data

    • Out Class

    Dropshipping Challenge

    Students target to get 1Cr+ in revenue by building their eCommerce ventures & selling on Amazon, BlinkIt & own websites.

    Download Dropshipping Report

    Global Immersion Programme

    Get the chance to travel and explore a new country and immerse yourself in its business, community, and socioeconomics.

    Download Japan GIP Report

    Shopfloor Internship

    Work on-ground with leading D2C brands to gain real-time consumer insights and solve business challenges firsthand.

    Download Internship Report

    In Class | Core + Elective Courses

    Foundational Courses

  • Managerial Behaviour - Understanding Human Psychology

  • Introduction to Operations - Building Systems from the Ground Up

  • Introduction to HRM - Managing People and Processes


    • Strategic Management

  • Corporate Strategies - Building Competitive Advantage

  • Corporate Governance -  How should Boards Work

    • Finance and Fintech

  • Corporate Finance - Maximising Shareholder Value

  • Venture Capital & FinTech - Decoding the Future of Finance

    • Product Management

  • Design Thinking and Prototyping - Building Customer-Centric Products

  • Product Analytics - Measuring Success with Metrics



    • Digital Marketing & Brand Management

  • Digital Marketing - How Instagram and YouTube Can Get You Customers

  • Understanding Consumer Behaviour-  Decoding Consumer Decision Making

    • Data Analytics

  • Introduction to Machine Learning -1 - Understanding Algorithms behind AI

    • Out Class

    Creator Challenge

    Transform your passion into profit by mastering the art of building and monetising personal brands as Creator-preneurs.

    Download Content Creator Challenge Report

    Local Consulting Challenge

    Tackle real-world business problems faced by local companies, develop innovative solutions, and gain hands-on consulting experience.

    Corporate Internships

    From EY to Zomato, work with top companies, apply classroom knowledge to practical challenges, and build a network that can jumpstart your professional journey.

    Download Internship Report

    In Class

    Foundational Courses

  • Intro to Blockchain, Metaverse & AI - Understanding New Age Technologies

  • Public Policy and Advocacy - How Businesses work with Government

    • Strategic Management

  • The Founder's Playbook - Strategic Scenarios & Real-World Application

  • Negotiation & Financial Acumen - Crafting Win-Win Solutions 

    • Finance and Fintech

  • Valuations & Financial Modelling - Finding a Company’s Worth

  • Markets, Portfolios & Prudence - The Investment Playbook




    • Product Management

  • Product Led Growth Strategies - Scaling 10X with Product

  • Product Strategy and Innovation - How Product Managers Innovate








    • Digital Marketing & Brand Management

  • Strategic Brand Management - How to Scale a Brand

  • Content Generation and Advertising - How Advertisers Influence Customers

    • Data Analytics

  • Big Data Analytics - Crunching Numbers to Find Patterns

  • Introduction to Machine Learning -2 - Applications in Business

    • Out Class

    Industry Treks

    Participate in industry-specific tours, and get the chance to interact with investors, founders, and executives of leading companies.

    Leadership Retreats

    Learn the art of leadership through a 7-10 day offsite retreat with uncertain and ever changing environments, and get trained in complex decision making.

    Entrepreneurship Capstone

    Experience a 0-to-1 journey from ideation to pitching real VCs, and get a grant to launch your own startups.

    Download Entrepreneurship Report

    Out Class

    Spend a Year at Illinois Tech

    Study for your 4th year in Chicago and graduate with a Bachelor of Science in Business Administration from Illinois Tech, US.

     Check Detailed Curriculum here

    Industry
    Immersion

    Dive into our modules where business management meets hands-on
    experience, giving you real-world skills that truly stand out.

    PhysicsWallah

    A deep dive into the workings of a billion-dollar ed-tech company

    The State Plate

    How a D2C brand transforms & thrives post-funding from Shark Tank

    CARS24

    Decoding the strategies & success stories fuelling this $3.3 billion company

    Payal Polyplast

    This factory exports plasticizers to more than 26 countries

    Sita Ram Diwan Chand

    Building an empire selling only chole bhature

    A Glimpse into the
    Weekly Schedule

    Learn Directly From
    Future Recruiters

    • Take courses with CXOs & MDs from top companies like Google, Microsoft, Amazon, PayPal, and academicians from renowned global institutes like Harvard, Wharton, Oxford and more.

    • Learn tech, business, strategy, product management and related core skills from highly-rated and experienced Professors-in-Residence.

    • Get 1-1 mentorship and shadow Masters in your preferred domain.

    • Get taught by Illinois Tech professors who bring cutting-edge research
      and global industry expertise       into the classroom.

    from Global Giants

    and Samsung    , along with renowned professors from top institutions.

    Priority Admissions Track
    For Students with Foreign University Admits

    In response to the global uncertainty and challenges students face with international admissions, we’re introducing the Priority Admissions Track at Masters' Union. This exclusive opportunity allows students studying abroad and those with offers from top global universities to skip the entrance test and GD rounds and fast-track directly to the interview stage with just their admit letter and SAT scores.

    Application Closed

    Enrollment Checklist
    Admissions Criteria

    01.
    Academics

    01.
    Academics

    Class X & XII Marks (Expected/Final) from one of the recognised boards including CBSE, ISC, IB, IGCSE etc. Both students with and without Mathematics in Class XII are eligible for the programme.

    02.
    Aptitude Score

    02.
    Aptitude Score

    Scores in Masters' Union Scholarship (Aptitude) Test/SAT/ACT.

    03.
    Extra Curricular Activities

    03.
    Extra Curricular Activities

    Applications would be evaluated for extracurricular achievements and projects.

    04.
    Communication & Creativity

    04.
    Communication & Creativity

    We will assess these qualities through application essays, in-person business simulation, and personal interviews.

    Who is an Ideal Candidate?

    At Masters' Union, your peers are exceptional, from international athletes and Shark Tank finalists to crore-portfolio investors. Our classroom is a melting pot of talent, ambition, and real-world impact. We seek students who stand out and have the drive to excel.

    Watch Episode Download Student Report

    Application
    Timeline & Process

    Rounds Dates Online Scholarship Test Interviews Results Application Fees Status Commencement Action
    Round 3 2nd Mar ‘26 -
    13th May ‘26    		 Apr/May ‘26 May/June ‘26 June ’26 INR 2,900/- Active Aug '26 Apply Now Apply Now
    Round 2 20th Dec ’25 -
    23rd Feb ‘26    		 Jan/Feb ‘26 Mar/Apr ‘26 15th Apr ‘26 INR 2,700/- Closed Aug '26 Apply Now Apply Now
    Round 1 15th Oct ’25 -
    5th Dec ‘25    		 Nov/Dec ‘25 Dec/Jan ‘25 14th
    Jan '26     		INR 2,400/- Closed Aug '26 Apply Now
    Round 2 Dec '24 - 28th Feb '25 Mar/Apr/May '25 Mar/Apr/May '25 INR 3,000/- Closed Aug '25 Apply Now
    Round 3 15th Mar '25 - 14th May '25 Mar/Apr/May '25 Mar/Apr/May '25 INR 3,500/- Closed Aug '25 Apply Now
    Round 4 18th May '25 - 16th Jul '25 Jun/Jul '25 Jun/Jul '25 INR 4,000/- Closed Aug '25 Apply Now

    Blueprint to a
    Standout Application

    Your application is more than just a form, it’s your first pitch to the admissions team. In this student-led video, Yashovardhan, a senior at Masters’ Union, shares the ultimate blueprint to crafting a stellar application, from structuring essays to avoiding common pitfalls.

    01.

    Complete the
    Application

    01.

    Complete the
    Application

    Requirements

    Essential Documents

    • School Marksheets of Class X and XI

    • 12th grade passing certificate for students graduating in either year 2024, 2025 or 2026

    Video Essay

    • Once you submit part 1 of the application and pay the application fees, you will have to submit your video within 7 days. Please note your video should be uploaded to Google drive and uploaded in part 2 of your application form. Kindly ensure that the video on Google drive is set to "Anyone with the link can view".

    • Video length: Please create a video that is no longer than two minutes in duration.

    • Hobbies: It would be helpful if applicants could pick interesting, unique points that will make them memorable for the admissions committee.

    • Reference Video: For tips on creating an engaging and impactful video essay, refer to this helpful guide here. here.

    Priority Admissions Track:

    • If you're studying abroad or have an offer from a top global university, you can fast-track your admission through our Priority Admissions Track. Skip the entrance test and group discussion rounds, and go directly to the interview stage. Simply submit your admit letter and SAT scores as part of your application to qualify for this expedited process.

    02.

    Shortlisting &
    Scholarship Aptitude Test

    02.

    Shortlisting & Scholarship Aptitude Test

    Requirements

    About the Online Scholarship Test

    • After getting confirmation on the shortlist, students will receive an online scholarship test from home. The test will be two hours long and will consist of questions on data analysis, numerical ability and logical reasoning. A practice test will also be provided. Students will get 14 days to finish the scholarship test. Based on the aptitude scores, students will be further shortlisted and contacted by the admissions team with information about their in-person simulation and interview. Preparation materials for the test can be found here.

    • Students also have the option to provide their JEE/IPMAT/SAT/CUET scores. The scholarship test becomes optional for those who have provided their scores.

    • Watch a student-led breakdown of a test strategy that can help you secure Masters’ Union Scholarship here.

    03.

    Schedule In-Person
    Business Simulation & Interview

    03.

    Schedule In-Person Business Simulation & Interview

    Requirements

    About In-person Business Simulation & Interview

    • The in-person business simulation will require candidates to role-play as a CXO of a company and solve its business challenges in a team of 5-6 applicants. The interview will be a 1-1 conversation with our faculty or senior leadership.

    • The in-person business simulation and interview will be conducted on the same day for a student. These will be scheduled across major cities in India, and over Zoom in exceptional cases.

    04.

    Admission
    Decision

    04.

    Admission Decision

    Requirements

    Status of Evaluation

    Once shortlisted applicants have submitted their application along with their aptitude scores, the admissions team will respond with their final decision and the students will fall into three categories:

    Accepted

    • Applicants who have been offered admission to Masters’ Union will receive their decision along with the financial structure for the upcoming programme.

    • They will have 7-21 days to confirm their admission through payment of the admission fee.

    Rejected

    • Applicants rejected by the admissions team can apply to the programme again in the next academic year.

    Waitlisted

    • Masters’ Union doesn’t maintain any ranked waitlist. Applicants placed in this category will have their admission dependent on the spots that might open up in the cohort.

    • The admission committee reconvenes to decide the results and promotion of candidates from the waitlisted to the accepted pool, regularly.

    Lateral Entry: Transfer to Masters’ Union

    Masters' Union offers a Lateral Entry pathway, allowing students in their first year at any other university to transfer seamlessly into our cutting-edge Undergraduate/IPM Programme in Technology and Business Management. Take the leap and elevate your academic experience with industry-aligned curriculum, expert mentorship, and real-world exposure. The online admission process is the same for all applicants.

    Apply Now

    Fee Structure

    Years Non-Refundable
    Admission Fee
    		 3 years in India + 1 year in US
    Tuition Fee
    Year 1 INR. 1,80,000/- INR 10,80,000/-
    Year 2 - INR 15,95,000/-
    Year 3 - INR 17,85,000/-
    Year 4 - INR 70,20,000/-*
    Total Fee INR 1,80,000/- INR 1,14,80,000/-

    *$78,000 converted to INR based on the 1:90 exchange rate. Actual amount will be dependent on the prevailing exchange rate at the time of study.

    Accommodation Type All Inclusive

    Single Sharing (Per Month) INR 48,500/-

    The above mentioned fee covers accommodation in Gurugram for the first three years of the programme.

    The amount mentioned for the hostel does not include electricity charges and taxes.

    Graduate with an Integrated PGP in Five Years

    Students can return to India and pursue an Integrated PGP, earning a PG Certificate from Masters’ Union in 5 years by opting in after Year 4.

    Prepare for Postgraduation at Leading Institutes

    Get personalized GMAT, GRE, GATE tutoring along with expert guidance on application preparation for MBA, MS, MPhil, and PhD programmes.

    Over 100+ Marquee Recruiters

    Top recruiters include Microsoft, Cisco, Google, Eightfold.ai, Krutrim, Amazon etc.

    Dedicated Career Support System

    From resume building and mock interviews to career outreach plans, our dedicated career coaches will hand-hold you throughout your journey.

    Opportunities to Work in the US

    Unlock stellar placement opportunities and H1B in the US.

    Download Placement Report

    10% of Our Students Raise VC Funds

    50+ startups incubated at Masters’ Union, including Bullspree (Raised $1M+), Eight.Network (Raised $1.8M+).

    Meet Unicorn Founders on Campus

    Learn what powers startup growth from founders like Peyush Bansal (Lenskart), Aman Gupta (boAt), Ghazal Alagh (Mamaearth) & Kunal Bahl (Snapdeal).

    ₹5 Crore Venture Fund for Student Startups

    Join a network where 80+ startups have already received funding from the student-led Masters’ Union Investment Fund.

    Student Entrepreneurs

    Explore what our students are building in the field of healthtech, e-commerce, AI, and more.

    Student Internship Experience

    Download Internship Report

    Jeet Shah

    Brand Marketing Intern, IndiGo

    Class of 2027

    “I landed my dream internship at IndiGo in my first year, where I contributed to launching their business class and loyalty program. This experience provided me with valuable insights into airline marketing and customer engagement. I have been passionate about aviation since childhood and my passion further deepened after meeting Dr. Anand Srinivasan, Co-founder of Akasa Air, during his tour at Masters’ Union, gaining firsthand industry insights”

    1/4

    Sabarna Jana

    Marketing Intern, Epigamia

    Class of 2027

    “During my internship at Epigamia, I was praised for my market research and identifying the right product-market fit – skills I honed in the marketing class taught by Mr. Bhupesh Manohar. I also analyzed raw data with precision with advanced excel, making the experience both practical and enriching.”

    2/4

    Gyan Banjan

    Business Development Intern, Zerope

    Class of 2027

    “My time at ZeroPe offered a front-row view of India's fintech transformation. I explored consumer behavior in healthcare spending and successfully converted a previously unreachable client. Tasked by Ashneer Grover to onboard 20 healthcare partners in two months, I applied the Need Gap strategy from one of our classes, which helped me close 44 leads in six weeks, earning the position of top performer and an extended offer to join the team full-time.”

    3/4

    Dhruv Lakra

    Product Intern, BluSmart

    Class of 2027

    “During my internship at BluSmart, I developed expertise in B2B sales, marketing, and social media. My experience in Dropshipping Mela helped me understand the business requirements quickly and I also honed skills in collaboration, analytics, and business building”

    4/4

    Roghan

    Founder’s Office Intern, Basil

    Class of 2027

    “I Interned at Basil’s Founder’s Office, where I crafted marketing strategies, closed deals with HP and Dyninno, and developed Basil Wallet as a product for these companies. With GTM strategies taught to us, I drove impactful marketing. The communication training from Harvard Professor Mihir Mankad equipped me to lead my team for two months”

    4/4

    Parents' Perspective
    The Masters’ Union Transformation

    Witnessing their child evolve into a confident, ambitious, and industry-ready professional is every parent’s dream. At Masters’ Union, our hands-on, immersive approach ensures that students not only excel academically but also develop real-world skills, leadership qualities, and an entrepreneurial mindset. Hear from parents who have seen this transformation first-hand.

    Inside the Union Stories

    Download Student Report

    Seerat Virk

    Jayshree Periwal High School, Jaipur

    Class of 2027

    “In my 1st year at Masters' Union, I am doing 3 internships, heading events, and building two businesses, simultaneously. In 6 months, I have honed so many skills.“

    1/10

    Shaily Dangi

    Indus International School, Bangalore

    Class of 2027

    “I believe that the value Masters' Union offers with their practitioners and industry experts as faculty distinguishes learning from others, even those abroad.“

    2/10

    Yashovardhan Karnawat

    Neerja Modi School, Jaipur

    Class of 2027

    "I have had the opportunity to learn from professors of Harvard, Wharton, and Stanford and even CEOs of unicorn companies. Their perspectives helped me understand how businesses scale globally.“

    3/10

    Chirag Naryani

    The Sanskaar Valley School, Bhopal

    Class of 2028

    “When I got to know about Masters' Union, I knew this was where I wanted to go. And trust me, seeing my skills and confidence grow in just a few months, I have never been more proud of my decision.“

    4/10

    Shabad Saxena

    Billabong High International School, Noida

    Class of 2028

    “The professors here are not just ordinary college professors; they are real-life business leaders who teach you via their own experiences and experiments in the business world.“

    5/10

    Sarala Birla Gyan Jyoti School, Guwahati

    Class of 2028

    Acharyakulam School, Haridwar

    Class of 2028

    7/10

    Atishay Jain

    Delhi Public School, Gurgaon

    Explore Programme

    Frequently Asked
    Questions

    • General
    • Video Essay
    • Scholarship (Aptitude) Test
    • Business Simulation/PI
    • Fees
    • Scholarship
    • Financing Options
    • Illinois Tech
    • Degree with IIM Bangalore

    Can students submit their documents/ certificates later if they need time to get it from school?

    Students can fill the form with certificates that are available. Later on, they can simply provide the remaining documents to the team as the application processes.

    Is it mandatory for students to submit their class XI marksheet?

    Yes, it is. However, if they don’t have the Class XI marksheet, they can upload an application explaining the reason for its unavailability at the time of filling out the application and attach the PDF in place of the marksheet.

    If a student has lost their X result (or any document) which is mandatory to upload, how can students fill the form?

    They can upload an application stating the reason for not having it under the 10th marksheet upload section. Ensure to send an official email at admission@mastersunion.org later, once the students obtain it.

    Can students edit the video essay?

    Yes, they can but it is not needed. We are judging students on their communication and confidence and not their video editing skills.

    Is there a sample video essay?

    Yes, here is a sample video.

    When will students get their application results and get shortlisted for the further round?

    Results can be expected to be announced anywhere between 15-30 days after the commencement of the deadline. Post receiving information on the shortlist, students will receive an online Scholarship (Aptitude) Test that they have to take online from home.

    What if a student has submitted the video but didn't get the Scholarship (Aptitude) Test mail yet?

    The email will be sent once the video is evaluated and students are selected. Results can be expected to be announced anywhere between 15-30 days after the submission of the video. Find more details here. here.

    What are the passing marks?

    We look into the profile holistically before selecting or rejecting a student. However, a score of 35 marks (out of a total of 45) is considered good.

    Can students submit their SAT Score instead of taking the Masters’ Union Scholarship (Aptitude) Test?

    Yes, students also have the option to provide their JEE/IPMAT/SAT/CUET scores. The Scholarship (Aptitude) Test becomes optional for those who have provided their scores and they will be shortlisted on the basis of the score. Please note that the scholarship test is not just for students looking for scholarships but is also an essential step of our admission process which enables us to assess the analytical capabilities of the student.

    How much time do students get to prepare for the Scholarship (Aptitude) Test?

    Students will get a 7-day period to finish the Scholarship (Aptitude) Test. Find more details here.

    Can students use calculators?

    Yes, students are allowed to use calculators. We suggest they keep a pen and paper handy with them during the test and a glass of water.

    Is there any negative marking?

    There is no negative marking.

    What is considered a good JEE/IPMAT/CUET percentile?

    Percentile greater than or equal to 90 is considered good.

    What is a good SAT score?

    Students who choose to submit their SAT scores, a score greater than or equal to 1500 is considered good. Please note the test score is one aspect of the application. Student profiles are evaluated holistically.

    Can students get an extension to appear for the Scholarship (Aptitude) test?

    They can write an email to the ugadmissions@mastersunion.org , attaching the specific documents explaining why an extension is needed. Extension can be granted in genuine cases, as per consideration via email.

    When will the results for the Scholarship (Aptitude) Test be announced?

    Results can be expected to be announced anywhere between 15-30 days after the test deadline.

    Is the business simulation/ personal interview conducted online or offline?

    The in-person business simulation and interview will be conducted on the same day for a student. These will be scheduled across major cities (Delhi, Bengaluru, Mumbai, Ahmedabad, Indore, Hyderabad, Chandigarh) in India and over Zoom in exceptional cases.

    What is GD or business simulation ? How to prepare for it?

    The in-person business simulation will require students to role-play as a CXO of a company and solve its business challenges in a team of 5-6 applicants. We will send them a case study to prepare for the business simulation round 3-4 days prior for preparation. In this round, they will be judged on their creativity, communication, and teamwork skills. They will be participating in a group discussion for 45 minutes followed by a presentation (without any slides).

    What happens in the personal interview, what kind of questions will be asked?

    The interview will be a one-on-one conversation with our faculty or senior leadership. The questions will be around their application and motivations for applying to Masters' Union. The goal is to know more about students and what they want to achieve at Masters' Union.

    Is there any detailed information about GD/PI ?

    • The overall process will take around 3 hours.

    • The business simulation will last 45 minutes followed by a 15 minute group presentation. Students will be working in teams of 4-6 students on a simulated business scenario, where students will be expected to make decisions, analyse data and present their findings.

    • The interview will be conducted by a senior leader or a faculty member at Masters’ Union and will be approximately 30 minutes long. They will be asked questions about their application including their achievements, essays and the video essay submitted.

    • Dress code for the event is smart casuals.

    When will the results be declared?

    The results are announced 30-45 days after the date of the interview. You can find more details here.

    Is there a firm deadline for payment of the admission fee?

    We provide students ample time till July of the academic year so that they can make their decision based on all the admission offers that they have. In case they still want an extension, the same can be granted based on an email request.

    What is the mode of payment for the fees?

    Mode of payment can be online, net banking, bank transfer or in some rare cases they can even send a cheque. Except for cash, we accept all payment methods.

    What is the fee payment schedule?

    Students can pay the fees semester wise. Starting every semester, they will be notified about the fees due by ugadmissions@mastersunion.org

    Is the study abroad programme included in the fee? Is there a different fee?

    The study abroad programme is not included in the standard tuition fee. Students opting for the 3+1 Dual Degree UG Programme will need to pay tuition fee as per Illinois Tech & living expenses of Chicago.

    Does Masters’ Union offer scholarships?

    At Masters’ Union we believe that financial constraints should not pose any hindrance to students’ academic aspirations. We have merit and need-based scholarships for all deserving candidates. Students scoring above 35 out of 45 marks in the Masters’ Union Scholarship Aptitude test get a scholarship which can range from 10% - 25% of the tuition fee depending on the student’s performance in the Scholarship (Aptitude)Test and the overall profile of the applicant. For candidates keen on scholarship, we encourage students to submit their application early to allow for our admission counsellors to thoroughly review their scholarship case before presenting it to the admissions committee at the time of evaluation.

    Is the scholarship for all four years or just for the first year?

    Scholarship is awarded for all four years. If a student receives a 20% scholarship, they will receive a 20% scholarship every semester on their respective fees. However, in cases of misconduct or rule violations, disciplinary action may be taken.

    Is the Masters’ Union scholarship applicable to the Illinois Tech fee?

    No, the Masters’ Union scholarship is not applicable to the Illinois Tech fee. However, students nominated and admitted to Illinois Tech through the 3+1 Dual Degree UG Programme may be eligible for a merit-based scholarship of up to 25% of tuition fees.

    To qualify, students must maintain a cumulative GPA of 3.0/4.0 or higher. The final decision on scholarship awards and eligibility is determined by Illinois Tech in accordance with its admission and scholarship policies.

    Are there any financing options?

    Yes, there are financial institutions that can provide loans for up to 95% of the programme fees to the admitted students. Read here for further details.

    Is the admissions process different for the 3+1 Dual Degree UG Programme with Illinois Institute of Technology?

    No, the admissions process for the 3+1 Dual Degree UG Programme is the same as the standard process. Applicants must apply through the regular admissions portal, and eligibility for the programme is assessed based on their academic performance, extracurricular activities, and an interview.

    Will students have the option to choose the duration of transfer?

    Currently, the programme is designed as a one-year transfer option. Students will spend their fourth year at Illinois Institute of Technology, completing the final year of their undergraduate degree as part of the 3+1 Dual Degree UG Programme.

    When can the student decide if they want to opt for the 3+1 Dual Degree UG Programme with Illinois Institute of Technology?

    Students are strongly encouraged to decide on opting for the 3+1 Dual Degree UG Programme before starting their undergraduate journey at Masters’ Union. Early confirmation ensures guaranteed seat allocation and allows for streamlined academic planning tailored to the programme's requirements.

    While students may still choose to opt for the programme during their third year, this is subject to seat availability, which will be communicated at that time. Confirming at the beginning ensures a smooth transition and optimal preparation for their transfer to Illinois Tech.

    Will the curriculum at Illinois Tech align with what students study at Masters’ Union?

    Yes, the curriculum has been designed to ensure a seamless transfer between the institutions. Students will continue to build on the foundational and advanced topics they study at Masters’ Union.

    Can students secure internships in the US while at Illinois Tech?

    Yes, students will have access to Illinois Tech’s career services, which include internship placements, job fairs, and networking events with global employers.

    What will I graduate with at the end of the programme?

    Upon completing the programme, you will be awarded a Professional Certificate for Undergraduate Programme in Tech & Business Management by Masters' Union. Additionally, you will have the option to enrol in IIM Bangalore to pursue a BBA in Digital Business and Entrepreneurship, provided you clear all the exams and the eligibility criteria as set by the IIM Bangalore.

    What is the difference between the Masters' Union certification and the IIM Bangalore degree?

    The Masters' Union certification focuses on an industry-integrated, tech-driven curriculum designed for business management professionals. The IIM Bangalore (BBA) degree is a traditional academic qualification offered with standard coursework and university examinations.

    What makes the Masters’ Union Programme a better choice than earning the same degree from IIM Bangalore?

    While the IIM Bangalore degree provides a strong academic foundation,Masters' Union offers real-world exposure from day one. Through outclasses and internships starting in Year 1, students gain hands-on experience with industry leaders from top companies like Microsoft, Google, and Zomato. This early exposure, combined with rigorous academic training, ensures that our students are not only academically strong but also industry-ready, with the highest CTC touching ₹61.5 LPA and an average CTC of ₹28.5 LPA, securing top-tier job offers even before graduation.

    Is this an online degree, is it authentic?

    Yes, the degree is awarded through IIM Bangalore’s online platform, making it an online degree. However, it holds the same recognition and value as any full-time IIM Bangalore degree.

    How well is this degree recognised for higher studies abroad?

    The degree awarded by IIM Bangalore is highly respected and recognised for higher studies abroad. With IIM Bangalore’s global reputation and the rigor of its curriculum, the degree holds significant international credibility, opening doors to top global universities and MBA programs for further education.

    Can I still pursue the Masters' Union certification if I don’t enrol in the IIM Bangalore degree?

    Yes, you can still complete the Masters' Union Undergraduate Programme in Tech and Business Management and earn the certification even if you choose not to enrol in the IIM Bangalore degree.
